songs.title: X-X-Box
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
…ks …ks …ks
(the sound hides at the END)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
X! X! …ks …ks!
What ENDS with X?
A box! B-O-X!
It's a box! (a box!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
X! X! …ks …ks!
What ENDS with X?
A fox! F-O-X!
It's a fox! (a fox!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
X! X! …ks …ks!
What ENDS with X?
Six! S-I-X!
It is six! (it's six!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
…ks is two sounds… /k/ and /s/…
ox… wax… taxi… (…ks hiding inside!)
X never starts — X hides at the end!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (does it end in X? NO!)
P-p-potato! Not X!
(…ks …ks)

[Final Hook — big]
Mix! M-I-X!
Mix it up, mix it up!
…ks …ks …ks!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🚨 X IS THE ODD ONE OUT — it is the one letter whose sound lives at the END of the word, and it is TWO sounds glued: /k/ + /s/ = 'ks'. So the frame FLIPS: not 'what BEGINS with X' but 'what ENDS with X?' — box, fox, six, ox. Say the two-sound blend crisply ('bo-ks', 'fo-ks'), never 'ex' (letter name). The whole song is the twist that X breaks the pattern.

songs.title: A Fox in a Box
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(peek… peek…)

[Hook]
A fox in a box! A fox in a box!
Who is in the box? (the fox! the fox!)
The fox is in the box!

[Verse 2]
Six in a box! Six in a box!
Who is in the box? (six! six!)
Six are in the box!

[Verse 3]
The cat is in the box!
The duck is in the box!
Who is in the box? (mix! a big mix!)
Mix it up in the box!

[Verse 4]
The ox is in the box?!
The ox is in the box?!
(too big! too big!)
The ox is STUCK in the box!

[Outro]
What is in the box?
(POTATO!)
A fox in a box…
(peek… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'The ___ is in the box.' cycling fox → six → cat → duck → ox → potato. Every -x word (fox, box, six, ox, mix) ends on the /ks/ blend. The Fox is the new cast member — sly, a hider, always peeking out of the box. Kids choir answers (the fox! the fox!).

spreads.artNote: A plain cardboard box alone under the spotlight, lid ajar, something bright watching from the dark slit inside.
The Fox's grand entrance — a red fox popping its head up out of the box, ears perked, sly grin. Cast member joins.

spreads.text: The fox is in the box.
spreads.artNote: Just two bright eyes and pointed ears peeking over the box rim, the rest of the fox hidden. Playful suspense.
Six little things crammed in the box beside the fox — six acorns, or the number 6 painted huge on the box side. Counting joke.

spreads.text: The cat is in the box.
spreads.artNote: The Cat (tabby) has squeezed in too, wedged shoulder to shoulder with the Fox, both looking out.

spreads.text: The duck is in the box!
spreads.artNote: The Duck (white) belly-flopping into the already-full box, sock still in bill, total chaos.

spreads.text: Mix! A big mix in the box!
spreads.artNote: The box overflowing — fox, cat, duck, tails and paws and wings all tangled in a big mix, box bulging.

spreads.text: The ox is in the box?!
spreads.artNote: An enormous ox trying to fit into the tiny box, only its rump in, box splitting at the seams. Absurd scale gag.
The empty box, everyone tumbled out — and the potato sits alone inside, spotlit. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture. The Fox peeks in from the edge.

sentences: A fox is in the box.
The cat is in the box.
The ox is in the box.
extras: The end-sound hunt: X hides at the END — clap the two sounds /k/+/s/ at the tail of box, fox, six, ox (no word this level STARTS with x)
Counting box: put SIX objects in the box and read 's-i-x', then mix them up and count again
Fox puppet joins the cast basket — count the friends: eight now, and the fox is the sly one who is always hiding

REUSED FROM WEEK 1 — a potato wearing tiny round glasses standing at a miniature whiteboard with a pointer, teaching, deadpan, ultra-realistic photograph, single subject centered, dramatic spotlight on deep forest-green backdrop, soft shadows, cinematic, slightly whimsical --ar 3:2 --style raw

teacherFocus: X is the odd letter out — its sound is TWO sounds glued (/k/+/s/) and it hides at the END of the word, not the start. Flip the game: 'what ENDS with x?' — box, fox, six, ox. Clap the two-sound tail. Put SIX objects in a box and read s-i-x. The Fox joins the cast as the sly hider — eight friends now.
